fields.name: browse
fields.description: Permission to view tickets

fields.name: submit
fields.description: Permission to submit tickets

fields.name: comment
fields.description: Permission to comment on tickets

fields.name: edit
fields.description: Permission to edit tickets

fields.name: triage
fields.description: Permission to resolve, re-open, transfer, or label tickets

types.name: ACLCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating access control list entries

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: ActivitySubscriptionCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating subscriptions

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

fields.name: supersededBy
fields.description: If this comment has been edited, this field points to the new revision.

types.name: DefaultACL
types.description:
These ACL policies are applied non-specifically, e.g. the default ACL for all
authenticated users.

fields.name: mailbox
fields.description: "jdoe@example.org" of "Jane Doe <jdoe@example.org>"

fields.name: name
fields.description: "Jane Doe" of "Jane Doe <jdoe@example.org>"

types.name: Event
types.description:
Represents an event which affects a ticket. Multiple changes can occur in a
single event, and are enumerated in the "changes" field.

types.name: EventCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating events

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description:
<service>:<service specific details...>
e.g. github:ddevault

fields.name: externalUrl
fields.description: The canonical external URL for this user, e.g. https://github.com/ddevault

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as "4") or integer (such as 4) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: ImportInput
types.description:
This is used for importing tickets from third-party services, and may only be
used by the tracker owner. It causes a ticket submission, update, or comment
to be attributed to an external user and appear as if it were submitted at a
specific time.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

fields.name: backgroundColor
fields.description: In CSS hexadecimal format

types.name: LabelCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating labels

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: createTracker
fields.description:
Creates a new bug tracker. If specified, the 'import' field specifies a
gzipped dump of a tracker to populate tickets from; see Tracker.export.

fields.name: updateTracker
fields.description: Updates an existing bug tracker

fields.name: deleteTracker
fields.description: Deletes a bug tracker

fields.name: updateUserACL
fields.description: Adds or updates the ACL for a specific user on a bug tracker

fields.name: updateTrackerACL
fields.description:
Updates the default ACL for a bug tracker, which applies to users and
senders for whom a more specific ACL does not exist.

fields.name: deleteACL
fields.description:
Removes a tracker ACL. Following this change, the default tracker ACL will
apply to this user.

fields.name: trackerSubscribe
fields.description: Subscribes to all email notifications for a tracker

fields.name: trackerUnsubscribe
fields.description:
Unsubscribes from email notifications for a tracker. If "tickets" is true,
also unsubscribe from all tickets on this tracker.

fields.name: ticketSubscribe
fields.description: Subscribes to all email notifications for a ticket

fields.name: ticketUnsubscribe
fields.description: Unsubscribes from email notifications for a ticket

fields.name: createLabel
fields.description:
Creates a new ticket label for a tracker. The colors must be in CSS
hexadecimal RGB format "#RRGGBB", i.e. "#000000" for black and "#FF0000" for
red.

fields.name: updateLabel
fields.description: Changes the name or colors for a label.

fields.name: deleteLabel
fields.description:
Deletes a label, removing it from any tickets which currently have it
applied.

fields.name: submitTicket
fields.description: Creates a new ticket.

fields.name: deleteTicket
fields.description: Deletes a ticket.

fields.name: updateTicket
fields.description: Updates a ticket's subject or body

fields.name: updateTicketStatus
fields.description: Updates the status or resolution of a ticket

fields.name: submitComment
fields.description: Submits a comment for a ticket

fields.name: assignUser
fields.description: Adds a user to the list of assigned users for a ticket

fields.name: unassignUser
fields.description: Removes a user from the list of assigned users for a ticket

fields.name: labelTicket
fields.description: Adds a label to the list of labels for a ticket

fields.name: unlabelTicket
fields.description: Removes a list from the list of labels for a ticket

fields.name: importTrackerDump
fields.description: Imports a gzipped JSON dump of tracker data

fields.name: createUserWebhook
fields.description:
Creates a new user webhook subscription. When an event from the
provided list of events occurs, the 'query' parameter (a GraphQL query)
will be evaluated and the results will be sent to the provided URL as the
body of an HTTP POST request. The list of events must include at least one
event, and no duplicates.

This query is evaluated in the webhook context, such that query { webhook }
may be used to access details of the event which trigged the webhook. The
query may not make any mutations.

fields.name: deleteUserWebhook
fields.description:
Deletes a user webhook. Any events already queued may still be
delivered after this request completes. Clients authenticated with a
personal access token may delete any webhook registered for their account,
but authorized OAuth 2.0 clients may only delete their own webhooks.
Manually deleting a webhook configured by a third-party client may cause
unexpected behavior with the third-party integration.

fields.name: createTrackerWebhook
fields.description: Creates a new tracker webhook.

fields.name: deleteTrackerWebhook
fields.description: Deletes a tracker webhook.

fields.name: createTicketWebhook
fields.description: Creates a new ticket webhook.

fields.name: deleteTicketWebhook
fields.description: Deletes a ticket webhook.

fields.name: deleteUser
fields.description: Deletes the authenticated user's account. Internal use only.

fields.name: updatePreferences
fields.description: Updates the user's preferences.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Returns API version information.

fields.name: me
fields.description: Returns the authenticated user.

fields.name: user
fields.description: Returns a specific user.

fields.name: tracker
fields.description: Looks up a tracker based on its resource ID.

fields.name: trackers
fields.description:
Returns trackers that the authenticated user has access to.

NOTE: in this version of the API, only trackers owned by the authenticated
user are returned, but in the future the default behavior will be to return
all trackers that the user either (1) has been given explicit access to via
ACLs or (2) has implicit access to either by ownership or group membership.

fields.name: events
fields.description:
List of events which the authenticated user is subscribed to or implicated
in, ordered by the event date (recent events first).

fields.name: subscriptions
fields.description: List of subscriptions of the authenticated user.

fields.name: userWebhooks
fields.description:
Returns a list of user webhook subscriptions. For clients
authenticated with a personal access token, this returns all webhooks
configured by all GraphQL clients for your account. For clients
authenticated with an OAuth 2.0 access token, this returns only webhooks
registered for your client.

fields.name: userWebhook
fields.description: Returns details of a user webhook subscription by its ID.

fields.name: webhook
fields.description:
Returns information about the webhook currently being processed. This is
not valid during normal queries over HTTP, and will return an error if used
outside of a webhook context.

fields.name: preferences
fields.description: Returns the preferences associated with this user account.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String`scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: SubmitCommentInput
types.description:
You may omit the status or resolution fields to leave them unchanged (or if
you do not have permission to change them). "resolution" is required if
status is RESOLVED.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
The ticket ID is unique within each tracker, but is not globally unique.
The first ticket opened on a given tracker will have ID 1, then 2, and so
on.

fields.name: ref
fields.description:
Canonical ticket reference string; may be used in comments to identify the
ticket from anywhere.

fields.name: subscription
fields.description:
If the authenticated user is subscribed to this ticket, this is that
subscription.

fields.name: webhooks
fields.description:
Returns a list of ticket webhook subscriptions. For clients
authenticated with a personal access token, this returns all webhooks
configured by all GraphQL clients for your account. For clients
authenticated with an OAuth 2.0 access token, this returns only webhooks
registered for your client.

fields.name: webhook
fields.description: Returns details of a ticket webhook subscription by its ID.

types.name: TicketCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating tickets

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: TicketSubscription
types.description:
A ticket subscription will notify a participant when activity occurs on a
ticket.

fields.name: subscription
fields.description:
If the authenticated user is subscribed to this tracker, this is that
subscription.

fields.name: acl
fields.description:
The access control list entry (or the default ACL) which describes the
authenticated user's permissions with respect to this tracker.

fields.name: export
fields.description:
Returns a URL from which the tracker owner may download a gzipped JSON
archive of the tracker.

fields.name: webhooks
fields.description:
Returns a list of tracker webhook subscriptions. For clients
authenticated with a personal access token, this returns all webhooks
configured by all GraphQL clients for your account. For clients
authenticated with an OAuth 2.0 access token, this returns only webhooks
registered for your client.

fields.name: webhook
fields.description: Returns details of a tracker webhook subscription by its ID.

types.name: TrackerACL
types.description:
These ACLs are configured for specific entities, and may be used to expand or
constrain the rights of a participant.

types.name: TrackerCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating trackers

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: TrackerInput
types.description: You may omit any fields to leave them unchanged.

types.name: TrackerSubscription
types.description:
A tracker subscription will notify a participant of all activity for a
tracker, including all new tickets and their events.

types.name: UpdateLabelInput
types.description: You may omit any fields to leave them unchanged.

types.name: UpdateStatusInput
types.description: "resolution" is required if status is RESOLVED.

types.name: UpdateTicketInput
types.description:
You may omit any fields to leave them unchanged. To remove the ticket body,
set it to null.

fields.name: tracker
fields.description: Returns a specific tracker.

fields.name: deprecationDate
fields.description:
If this API version is scheduled for deprecation, this is the date on which
it will stop working; or null if this API version is not scheduled for
deprecation.

fields.name: responseBody
fields.description:
These details are provided only after a response is received from the
remote server. If a response is sent whose Content-Type is not text/*, or
cannot be decoded as UTF-8, the response body will be null. It will be
truncated after 64 KiB.

types.name: WebhookDeliveryCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of webhook deliveries

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: client
fields.description:
If this webhook was registered by an authorized OAuth 2.0 client, this
field is non-null.

fields.name: deliveries
fields.description: All deliveries which have been sent to this webhook.

fields.name: sample
fields.description: Returns a sample payload for this subscription, for testing purposes

types.name: WebhookSubscriptionCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of webhook subscriptions

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.
